_comment: Hero stage. mode 'carousel' shows the slides below; mode 'video' shows an embed (set video.type to 'youtube' | 'vimeo' | 'file' and fill the matching field), and falls back to the carousel if no video source is given. See README > Hero & video for captions/transcript expectations.

slides.alt: Stephenson Hall at The University of Tulsa, the stone building that houses the AMDG lab, photographed at sunset.
Scanning electron microscope image series showing copper-coated Ti-6Al-4V powder particles forming sintering necks as temperature increases from 35 to 950 degrees Celsius.
Diagram of the group's research spanning basic science, core capabilities, and applied research in aerospace, biomedical, energy, defense, and manufacturing.
Figure showing how the group's characterization methods span nanoscale, microscale, and mesoscale length scales of sintering.
